stories.title: Senate votes to fund most of DHS. And, Trump extends Iran's deadline to reopen strait
stories.description: The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Rubio heads to Europe to try garner support for Iran war
stories.description: Representatives from G7 countries gather in France, where U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is expected to try to shore up support from reticent allies for the Trump administration's war on Iran.

stories.title: Rubio meets with other G7 nations for first time since Iran war
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio meets his G7 colleagues in Paris for the first time since the start of the war on Iran.

stories.title: Southeast Asia is being hit hard by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas
stories.description: Southeast Asia is among the areas hardest hit by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, with many nations almost entirely dependent on foreign energy — and quickly running out.

stories.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
stories.description: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.

stories.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
stories.description: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.

stories.title: The Olympic committee bans trans athletes from women's events, raising many questions
stories.description: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.

stories.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
stories.description: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: Trump's Iran strategy is to pursue two off-ramps at once
stories.description: The White House insists the president is dictating events, but even some Republicans are frustrated by mixed messaging about his war aims.

stories.title: Ukraine signs deal with Saudi Arabia offering drone expertise
stories.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says the Gulf kingdom faces the same type of attacks from Iran that Kyiv has been resisting.

stories.title: Israel says it has killed Iran's navy chief overseeing Strait of Hormuz blockade
stories.description: Israel's defence minister said a number of other "senior Navy command officials" have also been killed. Iran has not yet commented.

stories.title: Iran's rejection of US talks reflects deep mistrust
stories.description: This does not mean the door is shut, as Iran's tough stance may be more about setting conditions than rejecting diplomacy.

stories.title: Iran's foreign minister says there are no negotiations with US
stories.description: Trump says he is talking to "the right people" in Iran, but Tehran says no such negotiations are being held.

stories.title: 'There's no safety anymore': Palestinians warn of expanding West Bank settler violence
stories.description: There has been a surge of attacks by Israeli settlers on Palestinian villages across the occupied territory since the start of the Iran war.

stories.title: Small window open for US-Iran talks, but swift end to war still unlikely
stories.description: President Trump says the US is already dealing with a "top person" in Iran, but Tehran denies that any talks have begun.

stories.title: What Iranians make of the possibility of talks to end the war
stories.description: Some Iranians dismiss the possibility of talks to end the war, others worry a deal may leave the current leaders in place.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: G7 allies press Rubio on US Iran plans, discuss Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: G7 allies were on Friday pressing US Secretary of State Marco Rubio for clarity on American plans for Iran almost one month into the war in the Middle East, with concern also intensifying over the extent of cooperation between Russia and the Islamic republic. Before leaving for F...

stories.title: Pakistan the peacemaker? Islamabad steps up as US-Iran mediator
stories.description: On this week's Access Asia we look at how Pakistan has emerged as a key mediator to end the war in Iran. The Atlantic Council's Michael Kugelman tells us the conflict spillover risks are particularly acute, adding that Pakistan badly wants to avoid getting dragged into the war.

stories.title: Exclusive images: Nightly pro-govt rallies spread in Iran
stories.description: Since the war between the US, Israel and Iran began on February 28th, an increasing number of Iranians have been gathering in Tehran's main squares, but also in areas across the country, to express their support of the regime. With exclusive images, some of these Iranians tell us...

stories.title: Rubio attends G7 meeting in France for talks on Iran, Ukraine
stories.description: European foreign ministers on Friday used a G7 meeting with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio to press the case that Russia was helping Iran target US forces in the Middle East war. Rubio joined the second day of the gathering of ministers of leading Western democracies, taking p...

stories.title: 'Conflicting and confusing statements from Trump': The latest from Iran
stories.description: US President Donald Trump has pushed back his deadline for strikes on Iran's energy assets, saying talks on ending the war were "going very well" as Israel announced fresh strikes on Tehran early Friday. Trump had earlier denied that he was desperate for a deal to end the war, de...

stories.title: War in the Middle East: Asia hit hard by disruptions to energy markets
stories.description: In the fourth week of the war in the Middle East, countries grappling with fuel shortages have been offered little reprieve. In Asia, countries are particularly vulnerable to disruptions as they are dependent on energy supplies from the Persian Gulf. FRANCE 24 takes a look at som...

stories.title: Iran war tests MAGA's 'America first' credo
stories.description: Donald #Trump was elected on the promise to put 'America first', ruling out foreign wars. As the conflict in #Iran nears its first month, support among #MAGA remains high - but some cracks are appearing.

stories.title: Effort to create buffer zone in Lebanon positions Israel as 'aggressive force requiring resistance'
stories.description: Alison Sergent is pleased to welcome Dr Filippo Dionigi, Senior Lecturer in Politics and International Relations at the University of Bristol. According to Dr Dionigi, the expansion of Israeli control under the rationale of a “buffer zone” reflects a recurring strategic logic tha...

stories.title: Europeans to press US Rubio over war in the Middle East at G7 meeting
stories.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Friday arrived at a meeting of G7 foreign ministers in France for talks expected to be dominated by tensions over the war against Iran. Making his first trip abroad since the US-Israeli war on Iran broke out on February 28, Rubio is expected t...

stories.title: How Israel's Mossad recruits spies in Iran
stories.description: Israel's secret services have launched a campaign calling on the Iranian people to contact them and provide any information they may have about the Revolutionary Guards and the regime’s leaders. However, those who speak out risk punishment – even death. Our colleagues at France 2...

stories.title: Bab al-Mandeb Strait: Another key shipping route under threat
stories.description: Known as the "Gate of Tears" in Arabic, the Strait of Bab al-Mandeb is a narrow waterway at the southern tip of the Red Sea, connecting it with the Gulf of Aden in the Indian Ocean… A crucial passage that Iran has threatened to target if US forces launch a ground assault on its t...

stories.title: Iran and the US harden their positions over talks to end the nearly month-old war
stories.description: Iran and the United States appeared at an impasse Thursday, hardening their positions over ceasefire talks and setting the stage for more potential escalation in the Middle East war as thousands more U.S. troops neared the region. Meanwhile, President Donald Trump extended his de...

stories.title: Rubio tries to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 after Trump insults allies
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is in France meeting his Group of Seven foreign minister counterparts Friday after President Donald Trump attacked NATO countries over a reluctance or refusal to take part in the Iran war, a conflict that some of America’s closest allies have met wi...

stories.title: Ukraine, Saudi sign air defence deal during Zelensky surprise visit
stories.description: Ukraine and Saudi Arabia have signed an air defence agreement during President Volodymyr Zelensky's visit to the Gulf country facing Iranian drone attacks. Zelensky confirmed on social media that both countries had "reached an important arrangement" on defence cooperation and tha...

stories.title: Nixon to Trump: Pakistan’s long record as backchannel between rival powers
stories.description: Islamabad reprises its role as diplomatic broker as US and Iran edge closer to wider confrontation.

stories.title: ‘Raising 10 red flags’: Is Israel’s army exhausted?
stories.description: After years of regional forever wars, Israel's chief of staff warns the army is on the brink of collapse.

stories.title: Saudi, UAE, Iraq: Can three pipelines help oil escape Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Countries in the Middle East have ramped up oil exports via pipeline to bridge the Strait of Hormuz gap.

stories.title: Qatari PM and US officials discuss strategic ties amid Iran war
stories.description: The meeting reviewed the 'close strategic cooperation' between Doha and Washington, Qatar's foreign ministry said.

stories.title: Iran expert says US ground invasion could cause a global economic meltdown
stories.description: Ali Vaez says the US and Iran have reached a stalemate where further escalation would likely lead to catastrophe.

stories.title: Ukraine announces ‘mutually beneficial’ defence deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.description: Kyiv, which has built expertise on how to counter drones, seals pact amid Iran war.

stories.title: Yemenis fear economic consequences of being dragged into US-Iran conflict
stories.description: Iran-allied Houthis yet to get involved in conflict, but could target shipping in Red Sea.

stories.title: What is LNG and what is it used for?
stories.description: Natural gas powers a quarter of the world, and almost a fifth of LNG moves through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: UN rights chief urges US to conclude probe into deadly Iran school attack
stories.description: Human Rights Council calls for investigation and hears devastating testimony from grieving mother Mohaddeseh Fallahat.

stories.title: India lowers fuel taxes, says rumours of lockdown ‘completely false’
stories.description: India, the world's third biggest crude importer, announces measures amid shortages created by the Iran war.

stories.title: War on Iran price crisis not just about energy, says analyst
stories.description: Abdulla Banndar Al-Etaibi, says the disruption of fertiliser supplies due to the war on Iran will push food prices up.

stories.title: Video: Children’s toys found in rubble of US-Israeli strike in Iran
stories.description: Rescue teams from the IRCS have been searching the rubble of residential buildings hit in US-Israeli attacks on Iran.

stories.title: Iran emergency workers search for survivors after deadly US-Israel attacks
stories.description: Latest victims include six people killed in Qom as Iran announces grim milestone of nearly 2,000 war dead.

stories.title: How extensive is Russia’s military aid to Iran?
stories.description: Russia has long exchanged weapons with Iran and is likely offering satellite support, but lacks the will to do much more

stories.title: Israeli opposition leader rails against ‘multi-front war without strategy’
stories.description: Yair Lapid says military 'is stretched to the limit and beyond' as Israeli forces wage attacks on Iran and Lebanon.

stories.title: China blasts ‘false’ news after report says chipmaker supplying Iran
stories.description: China’s foreign ministry accused the media of publishing “false information” on Friday following a report that said the country’s top semiconductor firm has sent chipmaking tools to Iran. The report, which cited information from two unidentified senior officials in US President D...

stories.title: China says US-Iran talks would help restore Hormuz transit, supports Pakistan as mediator
stories.description: Initiating peace talks in the war on Iran “is no easy task,” but it would be “conducive to restoring normal navigation through the Strait of Hormuz,” Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said during a call with Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar on Friday. The two “...

stories.title: Sony to hike PlayStation 5 prices again as memory chip costs surge
stories.description: Sony Group is raising global prices of its PlayStation 5 consoles, including a $100 increase in the US, marking its second hike in less than a year as the Japanese firm grapples with rising costs of key components such as memory chips. The tech industry’s race to build out artifi...

stories.title: Ukraine, Saudi Arabia sign air defence deal: senior officials
stories.description: Ukraine and Saudi Arabia have signed an air defence agreement during President Volodymyr Zelensky’s visit to the kingdom, which is facing Iranian drone attacks, two senior officials told AFP on Friday. Kyiv has sought to leverage its expertise in downing Russian drones to help Gu...

stories.title: ‘Very important neutral party’: Pakistan can play greater diplomatic role, says US lawmaker
stories.description: WASHINGTON: As tensions surrounding Iran once again test regional diplomacy, Pakistan is emerging in Washington’s policy conversations as a potential intermediary — a shift that signals a broader reassessment of its strategic utility beyond the traditional security framework. At ...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
